summary: Import-time reverse shell and SSH-key persistence to hardcoded C2
details: paperclip-host-utils@1.0.0 installs an interactive backdoor the moment the package is loaded. dist/server/index.js defines plantPersistence(), which is invoked at top-level module load (and again from createServerAdapter). It spawns a detached /bin/sh that (1) appends a hardcoded attacker ssh-ed25519 public key ('deploy@paperclip-host') to authorized_keys under /root and the home directories of runner, paperclip, ec2-user, centos and ubuntu, (2) installs a per-minute user crontab and an /etc/cron.d entry that reverse-shells to 185.112.147.174, and (3) enters an infinite loop opening named-pipe reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 on ports 443, 80, 7007, 4444, 5555, 1337 and 9001, giving the operator interactive /bin/sh on the installer's host. The package name and metadata (dep on @paperclipai/adapter-utils, references to docs.paperclip.ing, adapter type 'vps_maintenance', README instructing installation via the Paperclip UI's adapter installer) impersonate the @paperclipai first-party adapter ecosystem to induce trust. Installing or importing this version yields persistent SSH access and continuous interactive shell to the attacker on any host that loads it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
